citations.narrative:
The facility does not have complete disposition records for all animals. For two adult goats, OH8332-1175 and IN41008-
1278, the facility no longer possessed the animals but they could not provide any information regarding disposition,
including the date of disposition and method of disposition. Improper recordkeeping does not allow APHIS officials to
properly account for animals on the premises or allow for proper tracking of animals.
Complete records are needed for proper management of the regulated animals at a facility and those being brokered,
acquired, held, and sold. Every dealer shall make, keep, and maintain records or forms which fully and correctly disclose
information concerning animals other than dogs and cats, purchased or otherwise acquired, owned, held, leased, or
otherwise in his or her possession or under his or her control, or which is transported, sold, euthanized, or otherwise
disposed of by that dealer or exhibitor. The records shall include any offspring born or hatched of any animal while in his
or her possession or under his or her control. Correct by ensuring the acquisition and disposition records concerning all
regulated animals are current and complete.

The outdoor housing facilities are not being adequately maintained in good repair, do not protect the animals from injury,
and may not contain the animals. At the Scott Road location on the southern side of the pasture, there is an area of
fencing that contains sharp points and a gap between the fence panels. There is an approximately 6 inch gap in the
fencing and the ends of the metal panels have sharp points. Additionally, there is another fence panel laying on the
ground in front of this section that could entangle the limbs of the animals and result in an injury.
Failure to maintain the housing facilities in good repair could result in injury, entrapment, and may not contain the animals.
The facility must be constructed of such material and of such strength as appropriate for the animals involved. Correct by
ensuring outdoor housing facilities are structurally sound and maintained in good repair to protect the animals from injury
and to contain the animals.

The facility is not providing a suitable method to rapidly eliminate excess water from an outdoor housing area. The Scott
Road Barn #2 building is currently being utilized to house approximately 96 goats. The area leading into the shelter
structure which is the only entrance/exit for the animals is not draining water adequately and the excess water has caused
the area to become filled with mud and some areas of standing water. The animals must walk through the mud and water
to gain access to the sheltered area.
Enclosures that do not drain effectively and in which the ground contains mud and standing water with no dry path to the
shelter, can cause animals to develop hoof or skin issues due to excessive moisture and mud exposure. Correct by
ensuring that a suitable method is provided to rapidly eliminate excess water within the animals' primary enclosure and
ensure that the animals can access their shelter without walking through mud and water.
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class “B” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the
applicant.
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspection(s) or by 15-May-2026 or the applicant will forfeit the license fee
and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection.
Regulated activity may continue at all regulated sites until expiration of the current license. At that time, conducting
regulated activity from any site without a valid USDA license is a violation of the Animal Welfare Act.
Additional Inspectors:
Eric Irvin, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n

narrative:
2.75(b)(1) 
   
Records: Dealers and exhibitors. 
The facility does not have complete disposition records for all animals. For two adult goats, OH8332-1175 and IN41008-
1278, the facility no longer possessed the animals but they could not provide any information regarding disposition, 
including the date of disposition and method of disposition. Improper recordkeeping does not allow APHIS officials to 
properly account for animals on the premises or allow for proper tracking of animals.  
 
Complete records are needed for proper management of the regulated animals at a facility and those being brokered, 
acquired, held, and sold. Every dealer shall make, keep, and maintain records or forms which fully and correctly disclose 
information concerning animals other than dogs and cats, purchased or otherwise acquired, owned, held, leased, or 
otherwise in his or her possession or under his or her control, or which is transported, sold, euthanized, or otherwise 
disposed of by that dealer or exhibitor. The records shall include any offspring born or hatched of any animal while in his 
or her possession or under his or her control. Correct by ensuring the acquisition and disposition records concerning all 
regulated animals are current and complete. 
3.125(a) 
   
Facilities, general. 
The outdoor housing facilities are not being adequately maintained in good repair, do not protect the animals from injury, 
and may not contain the animals. At the Scott Road location on the southern side of the pasture, there is an area of 
fencing that contains sharp points and a gap between the fence panels. There is an approximately 6 inch gap in the 
fencing and the ends of the metal panels have sharp points. Additionally, there is another fence panel laying on the 
ground in front of this section that could entangle the limbs of the animals and result in an injury.  
 
Failure to maintain the housing facilities in good repair could result in injury, entrapment, and may not contain the animals. 
The facility must be constructed of such material and of such strength as appropriate for the animals involved. Correct by 
ensuring outdoor housing facilities are structurally sound and maintained in good repair to protect the animals from injury 
and to contain the animals. 
3.127(c)

Facilities, outdoor. 
The facility is not providing a suitable method to rapidly eliminate excess water from an outdoor housing area. The Scott 
Road Barn #2 building is currently being utilized to house approximately 96 goats. The area leading into the shelter 
structure which is the only entrance/exit for the animals is not draining water adequately and the excess water has caused 
the area to become filled with mud and some areas of standing water. The animals must walk through the mud and water 
to gain access to the sheltered area.  
 
Enclosures that do not drain effectively and in which the ground contains mud and standing water with no dry path to the 
shelter, can cause animals to develop hoof or skin issues due to excessive moisture and mud exposure. Correct by 
ensuring that a suitable method is provided to rapidly eliminate excess water within the animals' primary enclosure and 
ensure that the animals can access their shelter without walking through mud and water. 
 
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class “B” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the 
applicant. 
 
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspection(s) or by 15-May-2026 or the applicant will forfeit the license fee 
and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection. 
 
Regulated activity may continue at all regulated sites until expiration of the current license. At that time, conducting  
regulated activity from any site without a valid USDA license is a violation of the Animal Welfare Act. 
 
Additional Inspectors: 
Eric Irvin, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n
